problem_summary: Diary capture currently depends too much on an explicit journaling instruction, each segment has only one theme field, and weekly context does not deliberately retrieve older related segments. This makes quick sentence capture less natural, limits cross-cutting search, and misses useful historical continuity during weekly reflection.
impact: A user will be able to enter a standalone personal experience, feeling, reflection, decision, or life-status sentence and have it routed into the existing diary draft/preview workflow. Each segment will retain one primary theme for narrative organization while also supporting multiple searchable tags. Weekly review context will include a small evidence-backed set of older related segments and may generate an optional reflection prompt when a meaningful pattern, change, or unfinished thread is found.
proposed_diff: 1. Update AGENTS.md and the Skill frontmatter/body so an unqualified declarative personal-life message defaults to diary capture; exclude direct questions, repository/task commands, and clearly non-diary requests. Preserve the existing safety contract: create the verbatim draft first, show a preview, and require explicit confirmation before final storage. 2. Extend the classifier/preview contract from a required single theme to a required primary theme plus optional tags, while accepting legacy {text, theme} payloads. Canonical preview output will use {text, theme, tags}, with tags deduplicated and excluding the primary theme. 3. Add a segment_tags junction table keyed by segment_id and theme_id. Keep segments.theme_id/theme_name as the primary theme for backward compatibility and Markdown headings. On initialization, idempotently backfill each existing primary theme into segment_tags. On confirmation, store the primary theme and every additional tag, resolving merged themes and rejecting inactive themes exactly as today. 4. Update FTS rebuilding, theme usage evidence, search result records, weekly context, Markdown rendering, storage-schema documentation, and README so all active/canonical primary themes and tags are searchable and visible. Theme governance must operate across primary themes and tags without rewriting confirmed originals or cleaned Markdown; reassign_segment remains primary-theme reassignment, and explicit tag mutations use add_segment_tag/remove_segment_tag proposal actions. 5. Add deterministic segment-level historical retrieval for weekly-context. Build queries from the current week's primary themes, tags, goal evidence, and meaningful terms; retrieve older confirmed segments using SQLite FTS5, canonical theme/tag overlap, local n-gram similarity, links, recency, novelty, and a token budget; exclude the current weekly period. Return dates, entry/segment IDs, scores, and compact evidence reasons. Do not add embeddings, a vector database, an API client, or an external model call. 6. Update the weekly Skill instructions to use retrieved historical connections when summarizing continuity and to ask an optional evidence-based prompt only when the retrieved material supports a pattern, changed view, repeated blocker, or unfinished thread. 7. Add migration, compatibility, multi-tag search, inactive/merged-tag governance, weekly historical-retrieval, no-false-prompt, and verbatim-preservation tests.
compatibility: Existing SQLite databases, confirmed Markdown, {text, theme} preview payloads, primary-theme headings, and confirmation rules remain valid. The migration is additive and idempotent. No confirmed original or cleaned diary is rewritten. The broader default trigger applies only to clearly personal declarative messages, so questions and operational requests retain normal assistant behavior.
tests: Verify legacy single-theme preview payloads still confirm and search correctly.
Verify one segment can have a primary theme plus multiple deduplicated tags and can be found by every active canonical tag.
Verify the schema migration/backfill is idempotent and leaves every tracked original and confirmed cleaned Markdown byte-identical.
Verify inactive and merged tags follow existing theme-governance rules and FTS is rebuilt with canonical active names.

Verify weekly-context retrieves relevant older segments, excludes the current weekly period, respects its budget, includes evidence metadata, and yields no historical prompt candidate when relevance is insufficient.
Verify the package still contains no OpenAI API key, client, embedding service, vector database, or external model dependency.
token_cost_change: increase slightly: multi-tag metadata and a bounded set of historical weekly evidence add context, offset by relevance, novelty, and token-budget stopping rules. Ordinary one-sentence capture remains compact.
